etymology.description: 八 (some liquid) + 酉 (alcohol vessel) -> depiction of fermented alcohol or decoration
etymology.interpretation: The character 酋 (qiú, "long-fermented alcohol or decoration") shows liquid dripping (八) from a vessel (酉), representing the concept of fermentation or decoration associated with an alcohol container. It is utilized in contexts involving aged or specially prepared alcohol.

examples.chinese: 他桌上的酒杯里装满了酋(酋)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhuō shàng de jiǔbēi lǐ zhuāng mǎn le qiú.
examples.english: His glass on the table was filled with long-fermented alcohol.

examples.chinese: 这瓶酋是经过多年酿造而成的(這瓶酋是經過多年釀造而成的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè píng qiú shì jīngguò duōnián niàngzào ér chéng de.
examples.english: This bottle of long-fermented alcohol was matured over many years.

examples.chinese: 他们在庆典上展示了传统的酋装饰(他們在慶典上展示了傳統的酋裝飾)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài qìngdiǎn shàng zhǎnshì le chuántǒng de qiú zhuāngshì.
examples.english: They displayed traditional decorations of qiú at the ceremony.
